title: RAG System Bottlenecks
concept: A RAG system is only as good as its retrieval; if the database returns irrelevant chunks, the LLM's answer will be poor. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Prompt Injection & Security.'

exercises.prompt: What does the acronym RAG stand for?
exercises.explain: RAG stands for Retrieval-Augmented Generation, meaning we first retrieve facts from a database, then generate an answer with an LLM.

cards.title: The Weakest Link
cards.body: Imagine asking a super-smart robot helper to build a toy, but you hand it a box of rusty nails and broken plastic. Even the best robot in the world can't make something great out of trash! That's the main bottleneck in RAG systems.

cards.title: The Retrieval Bottleneck
cards.body: A RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) system has two main parts: finding information (retrieval) and writing the answer (generation). The biggest bottleneck is retrieval: if the search engine brings back junk, the AI writer will produce junk. We call this "Garbage In, Garbage Out"!

cards.title: The Open-Book Exam
cards.body: Imagine you are taking an open-book exam, but your assistant hands you pages from a cooking book instead of a science textbook. No matter how smart you are, you will write a terrible science exam because you were given the wrong information! In RAG, the LLM is the student, and the database retrieval is the helper handing over the pages.

cards.title: A Bad Search Result
cards.body:
Let's say you ask a RAG chatbot: "What is our company's policy on remote work?"
If the database searches and retrieves a document about "Office Parking Spaces" instead, the LLM will confidently tell you about parking spots instead of answering your question about working from home!

cards.title: Myth: Just Get a Smarter AI
cards.body: People often think that if their AI chatbot gives a bad or incorrect answer, they need to buy a bigger, smarter Language Model (LLM). In reality, the LLM is usually fine; it's just being fed wrong or messy search results from the database! Fix the search, and you fix the system.

cards.title: Good vs. Bad Retrieval
cards.body: A comparison of how retrieval quality directly affects what the LLM generates.

left.text: The database retrieves documents about penguins when the user asked about lions. The LLM talks about penguins.

right.text: The database retrieves documents about lions. The LLM explains lion behavior perfectly.

exercises.prompt: Why is poor database retrieval considered a bottleneck in a RAG system?

options.text: The LLM's answer will be incorrect or low-quality.
The LLM will automatically search the live internet instead.
The system will crash and shut down.
The LLM will rewrite the database files.

exercises.explain: Because the LLM relies entirely on the retrieved context to answer specific questions, sending it irrelevant text will lead to incorrect or irrelevant answers.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A super-smart LLM can always generate the correct custom answer even if the database retrieves completely wrong information.
exercises.explain: Even the smartest LLM cannot generate a correct, specific answer if the database feeds it the wrong context document.

exercises.prompt: Complete this sentence about the main bottleneck in RAG systems.
exercises.sentence: A RAG system is only as good as its ____________ stage.
exercises.explain: If the database (retrieval) fails to find the right information, the generator (LLM) cannot write an accurate answer.

exercises.prompt: Identify which AI system is experiencing a retrieval bottleneck issue:

options.text: AI A: User asks 'How to bake bread?', Retrieval gets 'Bread recipe', AI answers 'Mix flour and water and bake.'
AI B: User asks 'How do dogs communicate?', Retrieval gets 'Cat behavior article', AI answers 'Dogs communicate by meowing.'
AI C: User asks 'Is it raining?', Retrieval gets 'Local weather forecast', AI answers 'Yes, it is raining.'

exercises.explain: AI B is the only one showing a retrieval bottleneck. It received the wrong document (about cats) for a dog question, which caused it to give a bad answer.

exercises.prompt: If the retrieval stage of a RAG system fetches completely irrelevant documents, what is the most likely outcome for the LLM's final answer?

options.text: The LLM will automatically fix the database errors using its own memory.
The LLM will generate a bad or hallucinated answer because it received bad data.
The database will automatically delete the LLM.
The RAG system will speed up and become more accurate.

exercises.explain: If the database retrieval fails to find the right information, the LLM has to guess or use outdated training data, which ruins the entire RAG pipeline. Next up: Prompt Injection & Security.
